title: Ike's Morning Digest
data_note: Built from publisher RSS metadata (titles + teaser text). `coverage_window_hours` is the default (40h); several feeds use a longer `rollingHours` (see `scripts/build-digest.mjs`) so rows align with what is still on each publisher’s listing pages. Commercial Observer keeps its retail category + hub supplement; TRD adds a New York–filtered global retail tag feed. PincusCo uses the live site feed only (`/category/retail/feed/` is stale). **CRE Daily** retail briefs come from `credaily.com/sectors/retail/feed/`. Open each link for the full article (subscriptions may apply in the browser).

section_overviews.commercial_observer:
Cashmere Brand Kujten Picks Acadia’s 92 Greene Street for Second U.S. Location: French cashmere brand Kujten has leased a second Manhattan location less than a year after opening its first U.S. flagship along Madison Avenue.

Bistro Pardon My French Sets Up Shop at Jeff Sutton’s 373 Broome Street: Ooh la la. French-inspired bistro Pardon My French is coming to Manhattan’s SoHo neighborhood after signing a 1,000-square-foot lease at 373 Broome Street , a property owned in a joint venture between Jeff Sutton ’s Wharton Properties and Gorjian Real Estate Group, according to broker Meridian Capital Group .

New Thai Restaurant to Open in 11K SF at 321 West 51st Street: One thing New York City doesn’t lack is a place to eat, and now there’s a new Thai restaurant from the restaurateurs behind Greenwich Village favorite Pranakhon coming to Manhattan’s Hell’s Kitchen neighborhood.

Medallion Financial Relocates to 9K SF at Hartz Group’s 667 Madison Avenue: Commercial finance company Medallion Financial has leased roughly 9,000 square feet at Hartz Group ’s 667 Madison Avenue . The lender, known for funding New York City’s yellow taxi cab fleet, signed an 11-year lease at the office building, located on the northeast corner of East 61st Street, Medallion confirmed.
section_overviews.cre_daily:
Chelsea Piers Fitness Leads May NYC Retail Lease Ranking: Chelsea Piers Fitness preleased 76,000 SF at Seaport’s 250 Water Street, marking the largest retail lease in NYC for May. New York City’s top 10 retail leases in May spanned fitness, fashion, dining, and collectibles, with notable deals from Marshalls, House of Spells, and Coach.

Drive-Thru Traffic Slides, Challenging Net Lease Retail: Short-duration, drive-thru visits to quick-service restaurants fell 6.8% year-over-year in May per Placer.ai. Full-service restaurants and experiential dining gained momentum even as drive-thru traffic declined.

URW Acquires Full Control Of San Diego’s Westfield UTC Mall: Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield is acquiring the remaining half of Westfield UTC for $705M. The 1M SF San Diego mall is set for further luxury expansion, with new brands arriving in 2026.

Toys and Collectibles Fuel Booming US Retail Foot Traffic: Toys and collectibles are rapidly boosting foot traffic and sales for US retailers, drawing demand from Gen Z, Millennials, and older buyers alike. Brands employ scarcity tactics and viral social trends, while global companies like Pop Mart and Miniso expand their US retail footprints to capitalize on the craze.
section_overviews.new_york_post:
K-Pop company DearU to open flagship store in Union Square: DearU, a global K-Pop company, will bring its US flagship and its first permanent brick-and-mortar store, to 24 Union Square East. Newmark’s Jeffrey Roseman and Drew Weiss are the exclusive leasing agents for the property’s owners, the S.

LA Graffiti Tower developer has insidious plot to make insane cash before tackling eyesore: Insiders: A bankruptcy court decision on the sale has been pushed back again to July 20 as the prospective buyer scrambles to convince the city hall that they can clean up the infamous three, graffiti-tagged towers.

Final design for NYC Penn Station’s redevelopment one step closer — including how much it could cost: The pre-development phase will run through 2027, during which time Amtrak and the development partners said they will conduct “robust” community engagement before the final plan is completed.

Booming town of Silverwood where homes are $400K and buyers have to sign ‘kindness’ waiver: The 9,000-acre project first grabbed headlines for its relatively affordable home prices — starting in the low $400,000s.

The pension system, also known as BERS, signed a 78,000-square-foot lease at 55 Water Street, expanding its footprint at the sprawling Financial District office tower and committing to the building for the next.

NYC’s top deals: UES townhouse trades for $22M: There were 172 transactions totaling $360 million filed in New York City records from 4 p.m. on Friday, June 19, through 4 p.m.

New development marketing guru Nancy Packes dies at 80: Nancy Packes, an early pioneer of new development marketing in New York City, died Thursday at the age of 80. A Bronx native, Packes graduated from New York University’s School of Law in 1970 and worked as a criminal trial lawyer for the next seven years.

items.headline: Cashmere Brand Kujten Picks Acadia’s 92 Greene Street for Second U.S. Location
items.summary: French cashmere brand Kujten has leased a second Manhattan location less than a year after opening its first U.S. flagship along Madison Avenue. The upcoming store at Acadia Realty Trust ’s 92 Greene Street , a midblock address bordered by Spring and Prince streets, sits in the heart of SoHo’s bustling shopping corridor. Kujten signed a long-term, 2,567-square-foot lease, with 1,531 square feet on the ground floor and 1,036 square feet on the lower level, according to landlord broker Newmark .…

items.headline: Bistro Pardon My French Sets Up Shop at Jeff Sutton’s 373 Broome Street
items.summary: Ooh la la. French-inspired bistro Pardon My French is coming to Manhattan’s SoHo neighborhood after signing a 1,000-square-foot lease at 373 Broome Street , a property owned in a joint venture between Jeff Sutton ’s Wharton Properties and Gorjian Real Estate Group, according to broker Meridian Capital Group . The asking rent for the ground-floor retail space was $225 per square foot. The length of the lease, which was signed at the end of April, was not disclosed.…

items.headline: Chelsea Piers Fitness Leads May NYC Retail Lease Ranking
items.summary: Chelsea Piers Fitness preleased 76,000 SF at Seaport’s 250 Water Street, marking the largest retail lease in NYC for May. New York City’s top 10 retail leases in May spanned fitness, fashion, dining, and collectibles, with notable deals from Marshalls, House of Spells, and Coach. The mix of tenants signals sustained demand across retail segments despite ongoing market headwinds and increased selectivity among landlords.…

items.headline: Drive-Thru Traffic Slides, Challenging Net Lease Retail
items.summary: Short-duration, drive-thru visits to quick-service restaurants fell 6.8% year-over-year in May per Placer.ai. Full-service restaurants and experiential dining gained momentum even as drive-thru traffic declined. CRE investors should reexamine assumptions about outparcel and net lease assets anchored by pure convenience demand.…

items.headline: URW Acquires Full Control Of San Diego’s Westfield UTC Mall
items.summary: Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield is acquiring the remaining half of Westfield UTC for $705M. The 1M SF San Diego mall is set for further luxury expansion, with new brands arriving in 2026. The deal reinforces URW’s pivot to hold top-performing US retail assets, defying the trend of mall selloffs. Key Takeaways Luxury Retail’s Resilience In San Diego Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield (URW) has agreed to buy out its joint venture partner’s 50% stake in San Diego’s Westfield UTC for $705M in cash and shares, according to Bisnow.…

items.headline: New Thai Restaurant to Open in 11K SF at 321 West 51st Street
items.summary: One thing New York City doesn’t lack is a place to eat, and now there’s a new Thai restaurant from the restaurateurs behind Greenwich Village favorite Pranakhon coming to Manhattan’s Hell’s Kitchen neighborhood. SG Thai , the hospitality group behind Pranakhon, located at 88 University Place near Union Square , will open a new eatery at Nicole Franques ’s 321 West 51st Street .…

items.headline: Toys and Collectibles Fuel Booming US Retail Foot Traffic
items.summary: Toys and collectibles are rapidly boosting foot traffic and sales for US retailers, drawing demand from Gen Z, Millennials, and older buyers alike. Brands employ scarcity tactics and viral social trends, while global companies like Pop Mart and Miniso expand their US retail footprints to capitalize on the craze. The collectibles surge is reshaping retail leasing dynamics—offering landlords, developers, and brokers new opportunities for high-frequency, intent-driven tenants.…

items.headline: Medallion Financial Relocates to 9K SF at Hartz Group’s 667 Madison Avenue
items.summary: Commercial finance company Medallion Financial has leased roughly 9,000 square feet at Hartz Group ’s 667 Madison Avenue . The lender, known for funding New York City’s yellow taxi cab fleet, signed an 11-year lease at the office building, located on the northeast corner of East 61st Street, Medallion confirmed. Asking rent in the 25-story tower runs from $120 to $190 per square foot, per the New York Post , which first reported on the transaction .…

items.headline: Content Creation Platform Spotter Takes 17K SF at Thor’s 25 West 39th Street
items.summary: Upload complete. Spotter , a platform that provides tools to help content creators post on YouTube , has signed a 17,000-square-foot lease at Thor Equities ’ 25 West 39th Street in Midtown. The lease spans the entire 14th floor of the 16-story office building one block south of Bryant Park , according to Crain’s New York Business , which first reported the deal. The deal is a three-year lease with an asking rent of approximately $70 per square foot.…

items.headline: BERS inks 78K sf lease at 55 Water in FiDi
items.summary: The New York City Board of Education Retirement System is making a long-term bet on Downtown Manhattan. The pension system, also known as BERS, signed a 78,000-square-foot lease at 55 Water Street, expanding its footprint at the sprawling Financial District office tower and committing to the building for the next three decades, according to CBRE. BERS already occupies 51,000 square feet on the building’s 50th floor as a subtenant.…

items.headline: NYC Education Retirement System Signs 78K-SF Lease at 55 Water Street
items.summary: A teacher’s retirement benefits agency is putting down long-term roots in Manhattan’s Financial District, Commercial Observer has learned. The New York City Board of Education Retirement Systems (BERS) signed a 30-year, 78,079-square-foot direct lease to remain on the 50th floor of 55 Water Street , a 52-story office building owned by Retirement Systems of Alabama , according to landlord broker CBRE .…

items.headline: US Small Business Tenants Face Profit Pressures in 2026
items.summary: Small business tenants are experiencing falling profitability, with a 1.3% year-over-year drop per Bank of America Institute’s May 2026 data. The war in Iran and surging energy costs have driven up construction and operating expenses, challenging lease renewal and expansion plans. CRE landlords could soon see higher vacancy and default rates from their largest tenant segment as financial stress accumulates.…

items.headline: France’s Junk Smash Burgers Enters U.S. With New Store in SoHo
items.summary: A French burger concept is opening up its first U.S. location in New York City’s SoHo neighborhood. Junk Smash Burgers , which launched in 2021 and has already spread across Paris and elsewhere in Western Europe, has signed a 10-year lease for 2,150 square feet at Thor Equities ’ 452 West Broadway , according to the landlord.…

items.headline: French Cafe L’Ami Pierre to Open at Midtown’s 400 Madison Avenue
items.summary: A French cafe by acclaimed restaurateur Pierre-Antoine Raberin is opening a second location in Midtown, Commercial Observer has learned. Raberin has signed a 2,400-square-foot lease at 400 Madison Avenue for the second outpost of L’Ami Pierre , according to tenant broker Judson CRE . The deal follows the exit of Raberin’s partner, Michelin -starred chef Eric Ripert , in May 2023 .…

items.headline: K-Pop company DearU to open flagship store in Union Square
items.summary: DearU, a global K-Pop company, will bring its US flagship and its first permanent brick-and-mortar store, to 24 Union Square East. Newmark’s Jeffrey Roseman and Drew Weiss are the exclusive leasing agents for the property’s owners, the S. Klein Family LLC.

items.headline: Blackstone Levels Up San Francisco Footprint With $279M Hyatt Regency Purchase
items.summary: “High on a hill, it calls to me,” Tony Bennett once sang of San Francisco, and he’s not the only one answering the enticing call of the City By the Bay. Blackstone just acquired the Hyatt Regency Embarcadero from Sunstone Hotel Investors for roughly $279 million, Commercial Observer can first report. The 821-key luxury waterfront hotel, at 5 Embarcadero Center , features a 17-story atrium and unobstructed views over the city and San Francisco Bay.…

items.summary: A bankruptcy court decision on the sale has been pushed back again to July 20 as the prospective buyer scrambles to convince the city hall that they can clean up the infamous three, graffiti-tagged towers.

items.headline: NYC’s top deals: UES townhouse trades for $22M
items.summary: There were 172 transactions totaling $360 million filed in New York City records from 4 p.m. on Friday, June 19, through 4 p.m. on Monday, June 22. Commercial: Olmstead Properties and Vertex Properties had the priciest commercial deal to hit records, with the closing of its purchase of the office building at 19 West 44th Street in Midtown. The seller was Savanna, who purchased the Class B, 303,000-square-foot, 16-story property in 2017 for $195 million. The company also spent $20 million upgrading it.…

items.headline: New development marketing guru Nancy Packes dies at 80
items.summary: Nancy Packes, an early pioneer of new development marketing in New York City, died Thursday at the age of 80. A Bronx native, Packes graduated from New York University’s School of Law in 1970 and worked as a criminal trial lawyer for the next seven years. Packes left law and in 1977 founded her own brokerage, Feathered Nest, which launched a more than three-decade career in real estate.…

items.headline: Soloviev eyes record $400 rent at nearly full 9 West 57th Street
items.summary: Manhattan office leasing is red-hot. But is it $400 a foot hot? Stefan Soloviev is marketing an office space at 9 West 57th Street that, if achieved, would shatter the $327 record rent his building achieved earlier this year. His Soloviev Group is eyeing $400 per square foot for the 11,155-square-foot space on the 50th Floor, one of two remaining spaces in the 1.5 million-square-foot tower.…

items.headline: Final design for NYC Penn Station’s redevelopment one step closer — including how much it could cost
items.summary: The pre-development phase will run through 2027, during which time Amtrak and the development partners said they will conduct “robust” community engagement before the final plan is completed.

items.headline: The challenge of cheap markets
items.summary: Studios for $1,050 a month, one-bedrooms for $1,310 and two-bedrooms for $1,525. Those are actual asking rents for “high end,” downtown, market-rate units in the Erdace Apartments, “the epitome of a luxe residence.” As you might guess, they are not in New York City. They’re in downtown Lake Charles, Louisiana. These markets might as well be on different planets.…

items.headline: Calvin Klein’s former East Hampton estate hits the market for $165M
items.summary: Another headline listing has hit the Hamptons. An East Hampton estate that once belonged to designer Calvin Klein is coming on the market asking $165 million, the Wall Street Journal reported. The roughly 8-acre home at 121 West End Road last sold for $85 million in 2021. The property has a 9,000-square-foot main house with seven bedrooms, along with a boathouse on the Georgica Pond and a red clay tennis court added by the seller.…

items.summary: The 9,000-acre project first grabbed headlines for its relatively affordable home prices — starting in the low $400,000s.
